## same() function

Deep comparison of two items

<b>Signature:</b>

```typescript
export declare function same<T>(a: T, b: T, unordered?: boolean): boolean;
```

## Parameters

|  Parameter | Type | Description |
|  --- | --- | --- |
|  a | T |  |
|  b | T |  |
|  unordered | boolean | <i>(Optional)</i> \[false\] Note: relevant only in array like iterables. objects, sets and maps are \*never checked for order\* of entries |

<b>Returns:</b>

boolean

## Remarks

items are "the same" if:

- a === b (for anything other than iterables &amp; POJO)

- a and b are POJO with same entries (order ignored, [same()](./common.same.md) used to compare values)

- a and b are Maps with same entries (order ignored, [same()](./common.same.md) used to compare keys &amp; values)

- a and b are Sets with same values (order ignored, [same()](./common.same.md) used to compare values)

- a and b are iterable (that are not Set or Map) with the same values, order checked if unordered=false (default)

## Example


```ts
same({a:1, b:2},{b:2, a:1}) // => true
same(new Map([['a',1],['b',2]]), new Map([['b',2],['a',1]])) // => true
same(new Map([[{a:1},{b:1}]]]), new Map([[{a:1},{b:1}]]])) // => true
same([0,1], [1,0]) // => false
same([0,1], [1,0], true) // => true
```
